data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Medtronic FQ3 earnings beat estimates, revenue misses; reaffirms FY25 outlook
Russia denies reports of OPEC+ oil supply hike delay
data.description:
It's not easy to decide oil strategy in the current climate. Fresh reports over the weekend suggested that OPEC+ delegates were considering a delay in restoring output, before they were denied by major producer Russia. While OPEC+ members meet monthly to set quotas for crude production, there will be more on the table next time around with oil (CL1:COM) recently giving up all its gains seen since the beginning of the year.

Crude reality: A promise by President Trump to "drill, baby, drill" will continue to see America produce more crude than any country on record, while the threat of tariffs on global growth has also weighed on crude. It's despite new sanctions on nations like Iran and commitments by the U.S. to cut the oil exports of the Islamic Republic. Meanwhile, many big countries like China and India are still getting their crude from shadow fleets, and members within OPEC+ have pumped more than their official quotas in a test that can challenge the cartel's unity.

OPEC+ is currently cutting 5.85M barrels per day, which is about 5.7% of global supply, and in December, the group extended its latest cuts through the first quarter of 2025. It marked the latest delay due to weaker demand and increasing outside supply, but some members are getting antsy, like the United Arab Emirates. It feels that it has done more of the heavy lifting amid a campaign for higher quotas, and any new delays could be met with resistance.

SA commentary: "There's a lot of spare capacity that can be deployed into this market," TD Wealth writes in Will Trump's Calls For OPEC To Lower Oil Prices Work? "I think OPEC doesn't really want to do that because they know a 10%, let's say, increase in supply would cause more than a percent reduction in price. And that would not necessarily be accretive for the revenue side for them. So, I think they're going to play it by ear, see how things go, and keep the market as balanced as they possibly can."

data.title: Valmont raises dividend by 13% to $0.68/share; announces $700M stock buyback authorization
data.description:
• Valmont (NYSE:VMI) declares $0.68/share quarterly dividend, 13.3% increase from prior dividend of $0.60.
  • Forward yield 0.83%
  • Payable April 15; for shareholders of record March 28; ex-div March 28.
  • The company anticipates increasing its dividend annually in the first quarter of each year, at a pace aligned with expected longer-term earnings growth. The board of directors has also approved a new $700M share repurchase authorization, representing approximately 10% of the company’s current market capitalization.
  • See VMI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: The GameStop plot thickens as the company looks to sell assets in France and Canada
data.description:
GameStop Corp. (NYSE:GME) announced on Tuesday that it intends to pursue a sale of its operations in France and Canada. The retailer said the decision was part of its evaluation of its international assets.

The development is interesting after GameStop (NYSE:GME) rallied last week amid buzz over a possible entry back into the crypto sector. CNBC reported that the retailer is eyeing investments in alternative asset classes, including crypto, and especially bitcoin.

Sources indicated that GameStop (GME) is still figuring out if the investment is sensible for the business. The report caught the attention of investors because GME had $4.6 billion in cash holdings at the end of Q3, compared to its current market cap of $12.1 billion.

Investors may remember that GameStop (GME) has a mixed history with cryptocurrency and blockchain initiatives in general. The company launched a self-custodial cryptocurrency wallet in 2022 that allowed customers to manage digital assets, including cryptocurrencies and non-fungible tokens. The move was part of GameStop's (GME) broader strategy to expand into the digital asset space.

Shares of GameStop (GME) moved 1.4% higher in premarket action on Tuesday.

data.title: Arcus Biosciences stock dips after pricing $150M offering
data.description:
Arcus Biosciences (NYSE:RCUS) shares dipped 4% after the company priced a stock offering to raise approximately $150 million.

The offering consists of 13,636,364 shares of common stock issued at $11.00 per share

Arcus intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to fund ongoing research and development activities, including the clinical development of casdatifan, a potential best-in-class HIF-2a inhibitor, manufacturing-related costs and other general corporate purposes, including working capital and operating expenses.

The offering is expected to close on or about February 19, 2025, subject to customary closing conditions.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Genuine Parts Company tops estimates despite softer end-market demand
data.description:
Genuine Parts Company (NYSE:GPC) reported sales increased 3.3% year-over-year to $5.6 billion in Q4. The company said the improvement was attributable to a 3.2% benefit from acquisitions, a net 0.6% favorable impact of foreign currency and other, partially offset by a 0.5% decrease in comparable sales. The quarter also included one additional selling day in the U.S. versus the same period of the prior year, which positively impacted sales growth by approximately 1.1%.

Industrial segment sales were down 1.2% to $2.1 billion, consisting of a 1.7% decrease in comparable sales and a 0.3% unfavorable impact of foreign currency, slightly offset by a 0.8% benefit from acquisitions. Global automotive segment sales were up 6.1% to $3.7 billion, consisting of a 4.6% benefit from acquisitions, a 1.3% favorable impact of foreign currency and other and a 0.2% increase in comparable sales.

Adjusted gross profit as a percentage of sales was up 50 basis points from a year ago to 36.9% of sales. EPS was reported at $1.61 vs. $1.55 consensus and $2.26 a year ago.

On the balance sheet, the company generated cash flow from operations of $1.3 billion for the twelve months of 2024. Genuine Parts (NYSE:GPC) ended the quarter and year with $2.0 billion in total liquidity, consisting of $1.5 billion availability on the revolving credit facility and $480 million in cash and cash equivalents.

Genuine Parts (GPC) noted that its results were impacted by macroeconomic conditions and softer end-market demand.

Looking ahead, Genuine Parts (GPC) sees 2025 total sales growth of 2% to 4% and EPS of $7.75 to $8.25 (midpoint $8.00) vs. $8.29 consensus.

Shares of Genuine Parts Company (GPC) were up 0.9% in premarket trading.

data.title: Waystar Non-GAAP EPS of $0.29 beats by $0.15, revenue of $244.1M beats by $11.81M
data.description:
• Waystar press release (NASDAQ:WAY): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.29 beats by $0.15.
  • Revenue of $244.1M (+18.1% Y/Y) beats by $11.81M.
  • 1,203 clients contributed over $100,000 in LTM revenue, up 15% year-over-year
  • Net revenue retention rate (NRR) of 110%
  • Subscription revenue of $121.6 million, up 18% year-over-year
  • Volume-based revenue of $121.2 million, up 19% year-over-year

As of February 18, 2025, Waystar provides the following guidance for its full fiscal year 2025.

  • Total revenue is expected to be between $1.0 billion and $1.016 billion
  • Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between $399 million and $407 million
  • Non-GAAP net income is expected to be between $237 million and $243 million
  • Diluted non-GAAP net income per share is expected to be between $1.29 and $1.32

data.title: Elon Musk-led DOGE firings said to have affected FDA staff reviewing Neuralink
data.description:
The FDA staffers reviewing Tesla (TSLA) CEO’s neurotechnology firm Neuralink were included as part of a broader set of federal layoffs announced by the U.S. government over the weekend in a restructuring process run by his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), Reuters reported.

Roughly 20 employees at the FDA’s office of neurological and physical medicine devices were affected by the job cuts, the news agency reported, citing two sources familiar with the matter. The layoffs also impacted several staff members who directly worked on Neuralink, the sources added.

The FDA, Musk, and the White House couldn’t be immediately available for comments.

The sources noted that the affected employees were not specifically targeted due to their involvement with investigations linked to Neuralink. However, the layoffs, widely reported by multiple media outlets over the weekend, are expected to affect the FDA’s ability to efficiently review medical device applications.

One of the sources said all affected employees were probationary, meaning they work under fewer legal protections and typically have a tenure of less than one or two years.

In January, Musk, who runs Tesla (TSLA) and his space exploration company SpaceX (SPACE), said that about 20–30 patients are expected to receive Neuralink-designed brain implants this year as part of clinical studies.

data.title: Blackbaud Non-GAAP EPS of $1.08 beats by $0.01, revenue of $302.23M misses by $1.18M
data.description:
• Blackbaud press release (NASDAQ:BLKB): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.08 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $302.23M (+2.4% Y/Y) misses by $1.18M.

  • Blackbaud today announced its 2025 full year financial guidance:

    • GAAP revenue of $1.115 billion to $1.125 billion vs $1.21B consensus
      • Organic revenue growth at constant currency of 4.5% to 5.4%
    • Non-GAAP adjusted EBITDA margin of 34.9% to 35.9%
    • Non-GAAP earnings per share of $4.16 to $4.35 vs $4.67 consensus
    • Non-GAAP adjusted free cash flow of $185 million to $195 million
